danger_signals.visual_form: Moving projectiles crossing screen from right to left
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring player intervention; missing five results in failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue-colored hostile entities popping up in garden arena
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy presence requiring rapid elimination before time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: Red-colored variant moles distinct from standard blue moles
danger_signals.meaning: Escalation threat—shooting red moles accelerates clock, increasing difficulty and threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Accumulating block structures reaching left edge of playing field
danger_signals.meaning: Failure state imminent; game ends when any complete row reaches far left

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual clock display in corner of Mole Patrol arena
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure indicator; running out of time triggers game over

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty rows and columns in Blastris games
safety_signals.meaning: Successful block elimination; progression toward next level

safety_signals.visual_form: Lines of blocks vanishing, remaining blocks shifting right
safety_signals.meaning: Successful completion of objective; blocks shifting indicates progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Garden cleared of blue mole entities before timer expires
safety_signals.meaning: Level completion; safe progression to next stage

safety_signals.visual_form: Crosshair/gun sight position matching target location
safety_signals.meaning: Ready state for action; proper aim before firing

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Communication screen featuring instructional lady in LazerBlazer modes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red moles (accelerator variant with distinct color coding)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Garden setting; moles emerge from holes in ground
Sky/space environment; missiles approach from right side of screen

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multi-colored blocks (Blastris B); monochromatic blocks (Blastris A)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Metallic-bordered unchangeable blocks (Blastris B) vs. standard blocks
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Grid-based arena; blocks scroll left-to-right or drop vertically

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White bullseye target on blue background; diagram of Super Scope displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game requires accurate shooting at center target; failing or moving scope after calibration causes aim inaccuracy throughout gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two main menu boxes labeled 'Blastris' and 'LazerBlazer' displayed; sub-menus with three game options each
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player selects via aiming and firing at desired game box

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven-row, ten-column grid with darkened lights indicator; blocks scroll horizontally; status bar shows score, level, shots available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Blocks stop upon hitting wall or other blocks; destroyed blocks trigger line-clearing cascades; progression requires clearing five lines per level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid arena with multi-colored single blocks; metallic-bordered unchangeable blocks visually distinct
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Blocks drop randomly; shooting blocks changes color (except metallic variants); metallic blocks produce distinct sound cue on impact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Garden arena with emerging mole holes; blue and red moles; clock timer in upper-right corner
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Moles pop up unpredictably; red moles visibly accelerate clock when shot; occasional rocket-riding mole crossover from Intercept mode grants bonus points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single-level garden arena; blue moles only; scoring displayed based on reaction time and accuracy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Emphasis on speed and precision; moles continue spawning; no time limit visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen with incoming missiles at varying distances; missile counter shows remaining allowed misses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Missiles approach from right side; player must judge depth and fire before missiles reach opposite edge; five misses = game over

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual escalation in speed, density, and complexity of threats across 30 levels per LazerBlazer game type
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Entity spawn rates increase; threat patterns become more complex; player must adapt aim and reaction timing

environmental_storytelling.example: Blue moles vs. red moles in Mole Patrol; red variant visually signals escalation mechanic (clock acceleration) distinct from elimination-only blue moles
Score, level, and shots-available displays in Blastris communicate resource management state; darkened lights indicator shows progress toward next level completion
Metallic-bordered blocks in Blastris B signal unchangeable game elements; matching sound cue (metallic clang) reinforces immutability
Rocket-riding mole appearing in Mole Patrol from LazerBlazer Intercept mode creates meta-narrative connection between game modes; bonus points reward player knowledge of game ecosystem
Block accumulation on left side of Blastris field signals failure state imminence; fill direction (left edge) communicates progression toward loss condition
Communication screen lady in LazerBlazer provides instructional presence, humanizing threat briefing and creating narrative framing for gameplay objectives

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Scrolls left-to-right until hitting wall or another block; stops upon collision
Destroyed on hit; no state change before destruction
Produces metallic clang sound; color unchanged; visual metallic sheen persists, signaling indestructibility
Changes color on impact; multiple color states possible; indicates multi-state entity
Pops up from hole location; remains visible until shot; requires elimination for stage completion
Pops up like blue mole; visually distinct red coloring; when shot, causes visible clock acceleration instead of elimination-only outcome
Flies across sky (presumed from Intercept mode); shooting grants bonus points; signals inter-game narrative connection
Travels across screen at varying perceived distances; requires depth-judged shot timing; missing five causes game over
Disappears; remaining blocks shift right one position; signals level progression mechanic

notes: Super Scope 6 is a light-gun shooter compilation that communicates game state primarily through visual block/entity rendering, color-coding threat variants, and UI status displays. The game employs minimal narrative framing except for the instructional avatar in LazerBlazer modes. Danger is communicated through accumulation (blocks filling grid), time pressure (clock display), and threat variant coloring (red vs. blue moles). Safety/progress is signaled through cleared lines, empty grids, and advancing level counts. The game's manifestation layer relies heavily on spatial positioning, color distinction, and sound cues (metallic clang) to communicate mechanical rules and entity properties. Cross-game references (rocket mole in Mole Patrol) create minimal narrative cohesion across the six-game collection.
